title: Rosa cardanica
taxonomy: Rosa cardanica is a species of rose in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Central Asia. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Rosa, which comprises approximately 150 species of roses. Rosa cardanica is further classified within the section Synstylae, which includes many species of fragrant, white-petaled roses. This classification places Rosa cardanica within the larger family of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
uses: Rosa cardanica is a species of rose that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rosa cardanica is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's flowers and leaves are used to make teas, infusions, and other remedies that are said to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.
benefits: Rosa cardanica, also known as the Serbo-Croatian rose or Cardan's rose, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rose's extracts are thought to have analgesic and antiseptic properties, making it potentially useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Rosa cardanica and its potential applications in modern medicine.

It is said to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making it a possible remedy for pain relief and muscle spasms. constituents: Rosa cardanica, also known as the Serbian rose, is a plant that contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils. These active constituents are responsible for its potential therapeutic and medicinal properties. The plant's flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rosa cardanica are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress, and improve skin health.
